shots.visual_intent: A clean vector-style optical bench fills the frame with subtle grid ticks; at center sits a light-cyan transparent triangular glass_prism with bright beveled edges, incident_white_ray enters thin and white from the left, and projection_screen stands upright on the right. Thin red, green, and blue output paths and their red_screen_dot, green_screen_dot, and blue_screen_dot are present as faint ghosted guides on the right, preserving the left-to-right layout without thick laser bars or sci-fi beams.
shots.narrative: A single white ray approaches a transparent glass prism on a teaching bench, with the screen waiting to record the separated spectrum.
shots.caption: A single white ray approaches a transparent glass prism on a teaching bench, with the screen waiting to record the separated spectrum.

relationships: projection_ray connects object_point -> pinhole -> projected_point
projected_point lies on image_plane
focal length bracket spans pinhole to image_plane when focal length is discussed
forbidden: Do not show projection rays detached from the pinhole.
Do not show projected points floating away from the image plane.
requirement_slots.natural_language_contract: Preserve geometric projection relationships as visible spatial constraints.

relationships: light/vector cues visibly connect to the lit surface point
highlight changes consistently with light/view geometry
forbidden: Do not show lighting terms without a visible lit surface relationship.

relationships: ray path visibly meets the reflecting/refracting surface
secondary rays branch from the interaction point when shown
forbidden: Do not draw rays floating independently of scene geometry.

relationships: incident_white_ray enters from the left toward glass_prism
glass_prism stays centered on dark_matte_optical_bench
surface_normal_entry is perpendicular to the left prism face at the entry point
surface_normal_exit is perpendicular to the right prism face at the exit point
spectrum_red_ray bends least, spectrum_green_ray stays between, and spectrum_blue_ray bends most
projection_screen stands to the right of glass_prism
red_screen_dot, green_screen_dot, and blue_screen_dot sit separated on projection_screen at the ray endpoints
forbidden: glass_prism replaced by a flat opaque triangle
projection_screen replaced by a blank glowing rectangle with no RGB dots
RGB rays collapsed into one white or rainbow smear
surface normals shown as generic axes unrelated to prism faces
formula blocks floating over the center of the prism
optical bench removed into a pure black void
decorative abstract particles substituted for the actual ray geometry

shots.visual_intent: The camera eases slightly closer to the prism entry face while the incident_white_ray strikes the left face, surface_normal_entry appears as a thin dashed line perpendicular to the glass surface, and a thin internal_ray bends downward toward that normal inside the cyan glass. Short attached labels sit beside the geometry: “incident ray” along the incoming line, “normal” beside surface_normal_entry, curved arcs marking theta_i outside the prism and theta_t inside it, with the Snell formula small near the lower bench edge and not covering the prism.
shots.narrative: At the first prism face, the incident ray bends toward the surface normal according to Snell's law.
shots.caption: At the first prism face, the incident ray bends toward the surface normal according to Snell's law.

shots.visual_intent: From the internal_ray region, three thin colored rays separate: spectrum_red_ray takes the shallowest path, spectrum_green_ray runs between, and spectrum_blue_ray bends most sharply through the prism and out of the exit face; surface_normal_exit appears as a second dashed line at the right prism face. The three rays animate to the projection_screen and land as separated red_screen_dot, green_screen_dot, and blue_screen_dot, while a small inequality label floats close to the prism edge with short attached “red,” “green,” and “blue” labels near their rays.
shots.narrative: Inside the prism, different refractive indices make red bend least, green bend more, and blue bend most.
shots.caption: Inside the prism, different refractive indices make red bend least, green bend more, and blue bend most.

shots.visual_intent: The camera returns to a balanced wide view of the full optical bench: incident_white_ray on the left, glass_prism centered, surface_normal_entry and surface_normal_exit dashed at their faces, spectrum_red_ray, spectrum_green_ray, and spectrum_blue_ray exiting rightward to the projection_screen. The screen holds three crisp separated colored dots or short streaks, with sparse attached labels “same incident ray” near the white ray, “different refractive index” near the colored fan, and “screen” beside the projection_screen, leaving all geometry unobstructed.
shots.narrative: The same incident ray produces different output directions because each color experiences a different refractive index.
shots.caption: The same incident ray produces different output directions because each color experiences a different refractive index.
